item.title: 90% 的软件工程都在集成 janky API，我喜欢它 | 90% of software engineering is integrating janky APIs, and I love it
item.description:
我今天偶然发现了这个 twitter 线程和相应的 Hacker News 评论：今天完成的 90% 的软件工程都在集成文档记录不佳的 API | 黑客新闻。 这个镜头根本没有引起我的共鸣。 我实际上认为这种态度是有毒的。

没有人说：“90% 的桥梁建造都是用不同质量的水泥制作形状”。 众所周知，土木工程涉及使用哪种水泥、设计合理的建筑、计算结构应力、组织大量劳动力、制定项目计划。 这是艰难的，具有挑战性的和有益的。 水泥的质量总是很差，因为它不是 unobtainium 可以神奇地塑造成指定的桥梁。

软件工程和我们自己的数字水泥也是如此：API。 事实上，结合粗糙的 API 来解决问题是如此具有挑战性和成就感，以至于我无法想象做其他任何事情。

两种类型的软件工程

这些天来，我正在编写一个 CRUD 电子商务应用程序，使用 PHP、wordpress 和 mysql。 在过去的 20 年里，我从事的工作从裸机嵌入式系统到量子密码学以太网桥，再到搜索引擎和“非谷歌规模”的数据工程。 这告诉我有两种截然不同的软件工程。

软件工程作为复制字节

一种是我所说的“复制字节”。 你得到输入字节，以某种方式与业务相关。 作为软件工程师，您的工作是将这些输入字节复制到指定的目的地。

例如，您收到客户的订单（行项目 + 送货地址），您必须将这些行项目转发给履行部门，并在此过程中记录一些日志条目。 您可能会在此过程中计算一些税或应用优惠券，稍微修改这些字节，但大致而言，您只是按照上帝设计的方式发送它们。

软件工程作为“发明”字节

另一种软件工程是我所说的“发明字节”，或“以非常复杂的方式转换字节”。 这是时髦算法、数据科学、数学和复杂事物的领域。 例如：获取输入的 3D 模型并输出一些 CAM 指令或在复杂的索引结构中搜索字符串。 通常，艰苦的思考是由大学或研究实验室的人完成的。

编写代码通常是工作中最微小的部分。 人们可能想知道这些“字节发明者”到底是不是软件工程师。 或许我们应该称他们为“3D模型转换工程师”，或者“搜索索引数据结构工程师”。 通常，他们的代码需要由知道如何缩进文件的人重写（或者说，将其从 hacky matlab 移植到 java）。

发明字节的 API 很奇怪。 坦率地说，我并没有真正涉足发明的字节，所以我不能对这些 API 的文档说太多。

您实际上可能会在整个职业生涯中复制字节

如果您的业务不是思考聪明的想法或制作电子游戏等漂亮的东西，而是从事赚钱的业务，那么您可能属于软件工程的“复制字节”类别。

要复制哪些字节，何时，何地，沿途添加哪些日志条目，如何扩展复制，如何确保复制的字节与其他复制的字节在事务上对齐，这些东西很复杂。 但它一直是乌龟，一个用于复制字节的 API 建立在另一个之上。 最终，您遇到了硬件，而男孩是用于复制字节的硬件 API，这完全是胡说八道。

您无需编写有趣的代码即可成为工程师

你可能认为这很愚蠢：你学会了如何平衡树，如何安排流程，以及如何做一些微分曲线几何； 如何编写编译器，如何设计数据库引擎，如何实现分布式共识； 你为什么不把那些漂亮的算法写成你日常工作的一部分呢？ 事情是，聪明的人已经这样做了，而且他们经常利用它做生意。 现在你可以为他们聪明的想法和勤奋的工作付钱，希望他们确实比你更聪明，然后再调用 API 来复制刚刚计算的任何字节。

人们出于各种不同的原因，以不同的方式，在不同的约束条件下想要传输数据。 这意味着即使是最好的书面 API 文档也必须具有足够的通用性，以涵盖其构建的用例，并且有一定的机会涵盖人们使用它的目的。 它很少完全适合您的用例。 因此，文档将不完美，并且可能根本无法涵盖您要查找的内容。

实际上，我认为 API 文档比以往任何时候都好很多。 我最近必须处理的所有 API 都带有 15 种语言的 copypasta、一个快速的可搜索索引、大量的教程、文章和视频，以及一个活跃的 github。 这比必须猜测放置在 PC-NFSD 假脱机上的文件需要多少空白填充才能让 COBOL 解析器满意要好得多。

通过结合糟糕的 API 解决问题是我做的最有成就感的事情

但这就是为什么我发现我的工作使用低于标准的 API 复制字节非常令人满意：

复制字节并不容易，尤其是在当今的分布式世界中。 弄清楚如何可靠、安全和大规模地做到这一点需要很多思考。 弄清楚将哪些数据传输给需要业务理解、人员知识和系统思维的人。 构建和维护解决方案需要产品和项目规划、管理团队、处理不断变化的需求。 它需要保持代码库的健康，指导其他工程师，与利益相关者保持一致，处理遗留代码。

我最终使用的 API 是我遇到的最少的问题。 有些很好，有些很糟糕，有些确实有问题，但它们与我工作的“工程”部分没有太大关系。 工程部分是关于用方框和箭头绘制图片。 方框是其他人的系统，箭头是其他人的 API。 我画了哪些箭头，它们指向哪里，沿着它们的小气泡包含什么，以及我选择使用哪些盒子，这就是我作为一名工程师所做的事情。

一个具体的例子：检查订单

作为一个难题的具体示例，通过将劣质 API 粘合在一起完全实现：在电子商务商店上完成订单。

  • 使用浏览器中的流畅 UX 向客户无缝显示支付过程和结帐状态（API：一些伪劣的 REST、一些 janky web socket 或其他、一些被黑客入侵的反应和一些可疑的 CSS。最终只是将状态代码从支付处理器铲到用户的浏览器，可能会添加一些花括号并转义一两个字符串。）
  • 记录事务并向业务和操作提供指标和分析（一些无意义的像素数据，一些腐烂的跟踪 JS，一些伪劣的 SQL，也许一些 janky JSON 进入 SQS 队列）
  • 处理付款信息、订单信息并将其显示在客户帐户部分/后端 CRM/fedex 中（我什至不打算在这里列出 API 的低劣程度）
  • 将送货地址转换为带有条形码的正确送货标签（与斑马和 ZPL 一起玩）
  • 将行项目分派到所需的履行中心，向仓库工作人员提供拣货信息，包括跟踪、指标、库存处理……

设计这样的东西，使其正确、高效地工作，具有可见性、可靠性、可扩展性，并满足业务需求，并为每个相关人员带来欢乐——现在这就是实际的工程。

你认为将 API 粘合在一起是工程吗？

你认为今天的软件工程很无聊吗？ 你讨厌将 API 粘合在一起吗？ 你认为什么构成了“真正的”软件工程？ 软件工程师理想的日常工作是什么？ 请务必在下方发表评论！



原文:

(Title photo: Ophelia photos - Creative Commons)

I stumbled upon this twitter thread and corresponding Hacker News comments today: 90% of software engineering done today is integrating poorly documented APIs | Hacker News. This take doesn’t resonate with me at all. I actually think this attitude is toxic.

Nobody says: "90% of building a bridge is making shapes out of cement of varying quality". It is well understood that civil engineering is about which cement to use, designing a sound architecture, computing structural stresses, organizing a large workforce, establishing a project plan. It is hard, challenging and rewarding. Cement will always be of poor quality, because it is not unobtainium that can magically be molded into the specified bridge.

The same goes for software engineering and our own digital cement: APIs. In fact, combining cruddy APIs to solve problems is so challenging and fulfilling that I can’t imagine doing anything else.

Two types of software engineering

These days, I am in the business of writing a CRUD e-commerce application, using PHP, wordpress and mysql of all things. In the past 20 years I've worked on anything from bare-metal embedded systems to quantum cryptography ethernet bridges to search engines and "not google-scale" data engineering. This has taught me that there are two very separate kinds of software engineering.

Software engineering as copying bytes

One is what I call "copying bytes". You get input bytes, relevant to the business in some way. As a software engineer, your job is copy these input bytes to their specified destinations.

For example, you receive an order from the customer (line items + shipping address), and you have to forward these line items to the fulfillment department, recording a few log entries along the way. You might calculate some tax or apply a coupon along the way, slightly modifying those bytes, but roughly, you are just sending them along the way god designed them.

Software engineering as “inventing” bytes

The other kind of software engineering is what I call "inventing bytes", or "transforming bytes in really complicated ways". This is the realm of funky algorithms, data science, mathematics, complicated stuff. For example: taking an input 3D model and outputting some CAM instructions or searching for a string in complicated index structures. Often, the hard thinking is done by people at universities or research labs.

Writing code is usually the tiniest part of the undertaking. One could wonder if these “byte inventors” are software engineers at all. Maybe we should call them "3D model transformation engineers", or "search index data structure engineers". Often, their code needs to be rewritten by someone who knows how to indent files (or say, port it from hacky matlab to java).

The APIs for inventing bytes are weird. Frankly, I don't really dabble in invented bytes, so I can't say too much as to the documentation of these APIs.

You might actually copy bytes your entire career

If you are not in the business of thinking clever ideas or making pretty things like video games, but in the business of making money, you probably fall into the "copying bytes" category of software engineering.

Which bytes to copy, when, where, what log entries to add along the way, how to scale the copying, how to ensure the copied bytes are transactionally aligned with other copied bytes, that stuff is complicated. But it's turtles all the way down, one API to copy bytes building upon another. Ultimately you hit the hardware, and boy are the hardware APIs to copy bytes utterly nonsense.

You don’t need to write interesting code to be an engineer

You might think it's inane: you learned how to balance trees, how to schedule processes, and how to do some differential curve geometry; how to write compilers, how design database engines and how to implement distributed consensus; why don't you get to write all those beautiful algorithms as part of your day job? Thing is, clever people have already done so, and often they made a business out of it. Now you can pay them for their clever thinking and their diligent work, hoping that they were indeed more clever than you, and go back to calling APIs to copy whatever bytes have just been computed.

People want to transfer data for all kind of different reasons, in different ways, with different constraints. That means that even the best written API documentation will have to be generic enough to cover the use case it was built for, and with some chance, what people are using it for. It will rarely exactly fit your use case. The documentation will thus not be perfect, and might not cover what you are looking for at all.

I actually think API documentation at large is miles better than it ever was. All the APIs I had to deal with lately come with copypasta for 15 languages, a snappy searchable index, a plethora of tutorials and articles and videos, an active github. This is miles better than having to guess how much whitespace padding a file placed on a PC-NFSD spool needs for the COBOL parser to be happy.

Solving problems by combining crappy APIs is the most fulfilling thing I do

But here's why I find my job copying bytes with subpar APIs singularly fulfilling:

Copying bytes is not easy, especially in today's distributed world. Figuring out how to do it reliably, securely and at scale requires a lot of thought. Figuring out which data to transfer to whom needs business understanding, knowledge of people and systems thinking. Building and maintaining a solution requires product and project planning, managing teams, dealing with changing requirements. It requires keeping a codebase healthy, mentoring other engineers, aligning with stakeholders, dealing with legacy code.

The APIs I ultimately use are the least of my problems. Some are nice, some are terrible, some are really problematic, but they don't have much to do with the "engineering" part of my job. The engineering part is all about drawing pictures with boxes and arrows. The boxes are other people's systems, the arrows are other people's APIs. What arrows I draw and where they point to and what the little bubbles along them contain, and which boxes I chose to use, that's what I do as an engineer.

A concrete example: checking out an order

As a concrete example of a hard problem, fully implemented by glueing together shoddy APIs: fulfilling an order on an e-commerce store.

  • seamlessly show payment process and checkout status to the customer using a slick UX in their browser (APIs: some shoddy REST, some janky web socket or whatever, some hacked react and some dubious CSS. Ultimately just shoveling the status code from the payment processor to the user's browser, potentially adding some curly braces and escaping a string or two.)
  • log the transaction and provide metrics and analytics to business and ops (some inane pixel data, some putrid tracking JS, some shoddy SQL, maybe some janky JSON going to a SQS queue)
  • process the payment information, order information and display it in the customers account section / backend CRM / fedex (I'm not even going to list how subpar the APIs are here)
  • transform the shipping address into a proper shipping label with barcode (have fun with zebra and ZPL)
  • dispatches the line items to the required fulfillment centers, provides picking information to the warehouse workers, with tracking, metrics, inventory handling, ...

Designing something like this so that it works correctly, efficiently, with visibility, reliability, scalability, and fulfills business needs, and sparks joy for every human involved – now that's actual engineering.

Do you think glueing together APIs is engineering?

Do you think software engineering today is boring? Do you hate glueing APIs together? What do you think constitutes "real" software engineering? What is the ideal day-to-day of a software engineer? Please be sure to comment below!
item.author: wesen

item.title: 您应该聘请自由职业者或应用程序开发公司 | What should you hire Freelancer or an App Development Company
item.description:
移动技术

发表于在开发移动应用程序时，组织必须考虑几个因素，包括

商业案例和目标受众。 如果一家公司想为其员工或客户开发移动应用程序，它应该找到最好的移动应用程序开发公司合作。 一家好的公司将帮助企业在不浪费时间或金钱的情况下实现其愿景。

  1. 您应该选择自由职业者还是代理机构？
    第一个问题可帮助您确定在雇用移动应用程序开发人员时所考虑的合作伙伴类型。 您应该聘请应用程序开发自由职业者还是应用程序开发机构？ 应用程序开发自由职业者是独立工作并提供咨询或合同服务的专业人士。 应用程序开发机构是提供应用程序开发和设计服务的企业。 该机构有工作人员和您项目所需的所有其他要求，因此他们很有可能按时完成您的项目。

  2. 他们的投资组合告诉你什么？
    当您与移动应用程序开发公司合作开发您的应用程序时，您将支持的关键内容。 您可以请他们指导您了解他们如何确保他们拥有适合您要求他们构建的内容的体验。 在您聘请开发人员之前，请下载他们之前在平台上的一些工作（iPhone、iPad、Android 构建了这些应用程序，以便您可以了解团队在他们所做的事情上是否具有专业知识。确保他们有相关类型的经验）您希望他们构建的应用程序 - 基本游戏与医疗记录应用程序非常不同。

  3. 他们为什么平台开发？
    在雇用开发公司时，必须确定该公司是否具有您计划使用的平台和语言的经验。 如果你正在构建一个 iOS 应用程序，那么一家只开发 Android 应用程序的公司将无济于事。 还要问公司是否支持跨平台开发。 从长远来看，这将为您节省时间和金钱。

  4. 公司的发展历程是怎样的？
    成功的移动应用程序是出色的移动应用程序开发策略的结果。

合适的移动应用程序开发人员应准备好：

确定应用程序将解决的问题 检查平台和设备/工具以协助维护应用程序的 UI/UX 列出开发原生或混合应用程序的方法 将您的想法转化为原型 结合适当的分析 Beta 测试应用程序以了解角色和评估用户体验 在部署应用程序时确保完整性 捕获指标以了解用户行为

确保他们有列出端到端提交过程和质量控制的文件。 此外，确保在出现任何前所未有的延误时采取的行动方案。

  1. 他们是否优先考虑用户体验？
    两者都在确保成功启动移动应用程序方面发挥着至关重要的作用——通过使功能易于访问和满足公众期望。 如果您的应用程序缺乏设计，您可能会失去人气和用户，这可能会彻底破坏您的项目。

  2. 公司的数据安全标准是什么？

根据 Varonis 的 2019 年全球数据风险报告，53% 的公司过去曾面临未受保护的数据和糟糕的网络安全实践。 他们存在隐私侵犯、系统信息泄露、存储不安全和部署不稳定等问题，这些都对移动应用程序的生命周期产生了负面影响。 这就是为什么选择使用安全代码构建移动应用程序的供应商至关重要的原因。

  1. 开发公司可以支持什么时间表？ 在将您的选择范围缩小到拥有正确经验并支持正确平台的移动开发公司之后，是时候讨论时间和金钱预算了。

您需要概述您对移动应用程序的需求和期望，以便供应商可以为您提供准确的估计，因此请确保您在执行此步骤之前已制定好计划。 您不必知道如何开发移动应用程序。 你只需要知道：

为什么需要应用程序 何时可能需要应用程序，如果有特定的截止日期 你希望应用程序做什么 你想如何将应用程序交付给人们或你想使用什么平台 你的目标受众是谁

大多数移动开发人员可以使用该信息来提供相当准确的开发时间表。 显然，一家公司能否满足您的时间线需求有助于您决定与哪个供应商合作。

  1. 什么是定价结构？

当您在考虑阶段讨论时间表和费用结构时，请务必了解您提供的所有信息的详细信息，以帮助确定时间表，以及您的供应商如何处理整个项目的价格变化。

  1. 他们如何与客户互动和合作？

应用程序开发公司应该能够检查上面的所有框，以便适合任何业务。 不过，重要的是要考虑您对什么感到满意。 您是在寻找大量的交流还是一些？ 这个问题列表可以帮助您做出决定：您是否希望定期检查您的移动应用项目的进度？ 在集成过程中，您是否需要内部 IT 专家与移动应用程序开发公司一起工作？ 或者你更喜欢少互动，只有在出现问题时才说话？

  1. 他们提供哪些法律保护？

在将您的应用程序投放市场之前，签署保密协议 (NDA) 或版权转让的书面合同非常重要。 该文档声明了您对应用程序、其内容和源代码的所有权。 您无法避免这一步，特别是如果您的应用程序包含自定义功能或业务 IP。

  1. 初始开发后您会获得什么类型的支持？

完成开发后，询问持续的支持和维护。 例如，如果您正在创建用于 Android 或 iOS 商店的移动应用程序，供应商是否会帮助您将其列出并优化以确保人们可以找到它？ 供应商是否会在集成和实施过程中提供故障排除？ 如果您将来想对应用程序进行更改，您将支付多少费用？



原文:

Mobulous Technology

Posted on May 2

When developing a mobile app, an organization must consider several factors, including

the business case and the target audience. If a company wants to develop a mobile app for its employees or customers, it should find the best mobile app development company to work with. A good firm will help the business bring its vision to life without wasting time or money.

  1. Should You Go For Freelancer or Agency?
    This first question helps you identify the types of partners that you look at when hiring a mobile app developer. Should you hire an app development freelancer, or an app development agency? An app development freelancer is a professional who works on their own and provides consulting or contract services. An app development agency is a business that offers app development and design services. The agency have staff and all other requirement that need for your project, so they have high probability of completing your project at time.

  2. What Does Their Portfolio Tell You?
    When you’re working with a mobile app development company to develop your app, the key things that you’ll be supporting. You can ask them to walk you through how they make sure that they have the right experience for what you’re asking them to build. Before you hire a developer, download some of their previous work on the platforms (iPhones, iPads, Android built those apps so that you can see whether the team has expertise in what they’re doing. Make sure they have experience with the type of app that you want them to build - basic games are very different from health care records apps.

  3. What Platforms Do They Develop For?
    When hiring a development company, it is essential to determine whether or not the company has experience with the platforms and languages that you plan to use. If you are building an iOS app, a company that only develops Android apps will be of little help. Also ask if the company supports cross-platform development. This will save you time and money in the long run.

  4. What Does the Company’s Development Process Look Like?
    A successful mobile app is a result of an excellent mobile app development strategy.

The right mobile app developer should be prepared to:

Identify the issue that the app will solve Check on platforms and devices/tools to assist Maintain UI/UX of the application Chalk out methods to develop a native or hybrid app Convert your ideas into a prototype Incorporate appropriate analytics Beta test the app to know the persona and evaluate the user experience Ensure integrity while deploying the app Capture metrics to understand user behavior

Make sure that they have documents that list out the process of end-to-end submission and well as quality control. Also, ensure the course of action to take in case of any unprecedented delays.

  1. Do They Prioritize User Experience?
    UI and UX are integral parts of a good design. Both play a crucial role in ensuring the successful launch of mobile apps—by making features accessible and meeting public expectations. If you have an app that lacks in design, you may lose popularity and users, which could ruin your project altogether.

  2. What Are the Company’s Data Security Standards?

As per Varonis’ 2019 Global Data Risk Report, 53% of companies have faced unprotected data and poor cybersecurity practices in the past. They had privacy violations, system information leaks, insecure storage, and unstable deployment, which negatively affected mobile apps’ lifespan. This is why it is crucial for you to choose a vendor that builds mobile apps with secure code.

  1. What Timeline Can the Development Company Support? After narrowing down your options to mobile development companies that have the right experience and support the right platforms, it’s time to talk about time and money budgets.

You’ll need to outline your needs and expectations for the mobile app so the vendor can give you accurate estimates, so ensure you have that mapped out before you get to this step. You don’t have to know the how of developing the mobile app. You just need to know:

Why you need the app When you might need the app, if there’s a specific deadline What you want the app to do How you want to deliver the app to people or what platforms you want to use Who your target audience is

Most mobile developers can use that information to provide a fairly accurate timeline for development. Obviously, whether a company can meet your timeline needs helps you decide which vendor to work with.

  1. What is the Pricing Structure?

While you’re discussing timelines and fee structures during the consideration phase, make sure to get the details on all the information you provided to help determine timelines, as well as how your vendor handles price changes throughout the project.

  1. How Do They Interact and Partner with Clients?

An app development company should be able to check off all the boxes above in order to be the right fit for any business. It is important to consider what you are comfortable with, though. Are you looking for a lot of communication or some? This list of questions can help you make a decision: Will you want regular progress check-ins on your mobile app project? Do you need internal IT experts to work alongside the mobile app development company during the integration process? Or would you prefer less interaction and only talk once there is a problem?

  1. What Legal Protections Do They Provide?

It is important to sign a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) or a written contract for copyright assignment before releasing your app into the market. The document declares your ownership of the app, its content, and the source code. You cannot manage to avoid this step, particularly, if your application holds customized features or business IP.

  1. What Type of Support Do You Get After Initial Development?

When you finish development, ask about ongoing support and maintenance. For example, if you’re creating a mobile app for use in the Android or iOS stores, does the vendor help you get it listed and optimized to ensure people can find it? Will the vendor provide troubleshooting during integration and implementation processes? How much will you pay if you want to make changes to the app in the future?
item.author: mobuousdev

item.title: Remix 中的资源路由 | Resource routes in Remix
item.description:
到目前为止，我们一直在研究 Remix 中的路由，它们始终是基于 HTML 的输出路由。

这意味着它们会渲染一些 HTML，但是如果我们想要不同的路由类型会发生什么？

一些例子：

  • 图片
  • 不用担心。 有多种方法可以实现端点 URL 以创建这些路由。

    您可以创建以下任何格式：

     - app/routes/reports/$id/pdf.ts - app/routes/reports/$id[.pdf].ts - app/routes/reports/$id[.]pdf.ts - app/routes/reports.$id[.]pdf.ts

    所有这些都将具有相同的输出格式：

    http://yourwebsite.com/reports/id.pdf

    但是让我们看看如何将它输出为文件而不是纯 HTML。 为了实现这一点，我们必须使用 loader 函数来改变我们的输出，如下所示： 

 export async function loader ({ params }) { const report = await getReport ( params . id ); const pdf = await generateReportPDF ( report ); return new Response ( pdf , { status : 200 , headers : { ' Content-Type ' : ' application/pdf ' , }, }); }

这个例子使用了一些我们没有的函数，包括getReport和generateReportPDF函数。
这意味着它只是您如何实现它的一个示例（取自官方 Remix 文档）。

使用不同的类型

假设我们想要使用 JSON 格式？

让我们将文档更改为以下格式：

app/routes/reports.$id[.]pdf.ts

 import { json } from ' @remix-run/node ' ; export async function loader ({ params }) { return json ({ foo : params . id }); }

在此示例中，我们将动态 URL 中的 ID 格式化为 JSON 对象。

这很酷。 这样，我们就可以在 Remix 中支持所有这些不同的格式。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系

阿布舍克·贾斯瓦尔—— Apr 16 4月16日



原文:

So far, we have been looking at routes in Remix, and they are always HTML-based output routes.

It means they render some HTML, but what happens if we want to have a different route type?

Some examples:

  • JSON endpoints
  • Images
  • PDF files

Don't worry. Remix got your back on that, as we can use their fantastic resource routes for this.

There are multiple ways of achieving the endpoint URL to create these routes.

You can create any of the following formats:

- app/routes/reports/$id/pdf.ts
- app/routes/reports/$id[.pdf].ts
- app/routes/reports/$id[.]pdf.ts
- app/routes/reports.$id[.]pdf.ts

All of these will have the same output format:

http://yourwebsite.com/reports/id.pdf

But let's see how we can output it as a file rather than plain HTML. To achieve this we have to use the loader function to change our output like this:

export async function loader({ params }) { const report = await getReport(params.id); const pdf = await generateReportPDF(report); return new Response(pdf, { status: 200, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/pdf', }, });
}

This example uses some functions we don't have, including the getReport and generateReportPDF functions.
This means it's only an example of how you can achieve it (taken from the official Remix docs).

Using different types

Let's say we instead want to have a JSON format?

Let's change our document to the following format:

app/routes/reports.$id[.]pdf.ts

import { json } from '@remix-run/node'; export async function loader({ params }) { return json({ foo: params.id });
}

We format the ID from our dynamic URL into a JSON object in this example.

This is pretty cool. This way, we can support all these different formats in Remix.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Abhishek jaiswal - Apr 16
item.author: dailydevtips1

item.title: 作为一名软件工程师，我在第一份工作中犯了五个最大的错误。 | Five biggest mistakes I made in my first job as a software engineer.
item.description:
当我作为软件工程师加入我的第一家公司时，我在那里犯了一些错误，这些错误如下：

  1. 快速解决问题：总是试图尽快解决问题，所以我的经理对我很满意。 但是，快速解决问题会在未来产生更多问题。

抱怨其他同事不利于你的形象。

了解其他观点非常重要。

我在第一份工作中犯了这五个错误，我以为我做得很好，但后来意识到我完全错了。



原文:

When I joined my first company as software engineer I made few mistake there, Those are listed below:

  1. Solving issues in a fast manner: Always trying to resolve issues as quickly as possible, So my manager gets happy with me. However, solving issues in a fast manner creates more issues in the future.

2) Complaining about coworkers: Whenever I get the chance, I always try to complain to my manager about the quality of other coworkers' code. Complaining about other coworkers is bad for your image.

3.) Not thinking in terms of the bigger picture: I was always concerned with completing tasks, but I realized that understanding the project in its larger context would motivate me.

4.) Didn't focus on soft skills: As a programmer, I thought writing codes was important for me, but I was wrong soft skills are essential to being successful in the corporate world.

5.) Not listening to others: I always tried to give my opinion, but I was wrong. Understanding other perspectives are very important.

I made these five mistakes in my first job, I thought I was doing well but later realized I was completely wrong.
item.author: yourmdsarfaraj

item.title: 将 Rails 6 升级到 Rails 7 并安装 Bootstrap v5 | Upgrade Rails 6 to Rails 7 and install Bootstrap v5
item.description:
让我们先将 Rails 升级到 v7

将 Rails 升级到 v7

 // In Gemfile gem 'rails', '~> 7.0.0' # Change to this
 // In the terminal $ bundle update

升级 Rails 包

在package.json中，更新所有 rails 包

$ yarn upgrade @rails/actioncable --latest $ yarn upgrade @rails/activestorage --latest $ yarn upgrade @rails/ujs --latest $ yarn upgrade @rails/webpacker --latest

运行更新 Rails 任务

// In terminal $ bin/rails app:update

验证框架默认值

// In config/application.rb # old config.load_defaults 6.1 # Change to 7.0 # new config.load_defaults 7.0 # After change to 7.0

如果您遇到此错误

PG::UndefinedTable: ERROR: relation "active_storage_blobs" does not exist

然后执行以下操作：

 // In terminal $ rails active_storage:install

它将在db/migrate创建一个create_active_storage_tables.active_storage.rb文件，然后

// In terminal $ rails db:migrate

然后，返回验证框架默认值部分，然后继续。
阅读此stackoverflow 解决方案

现在，我们将继续安装 bootstrap v5。

将bootstrap和popperjs/core安装到依赖项

// In terminal $ yarn add bootstrap and popperjs/core

因此，您的package.json文件应该添加以下两行：

 "dependencies": { "@popperjs/core": "^2.11.5", "bootstrap": "^5.1.3" }

注意：这里的版本可能不同，但由于我们的目的是安装 bootstrap v5，它必须类似于5.xx 。

将default的extract_css更改为true

 // In config/webpacker.yml default: &default extract_css: true # Change to true

将stylesheet_pack_tag更改为stylesheet_link_tag

由于原始应用程序仍然使用 Rails v6 的配置，我们将使用 Webpacker 代替 Sprockets/Asset Pipeline

 // In app/views/layouts/<filename>.html.erb // My <filename> is application, so in app/views/layouts/application.html.erb <%= stylesheet_link_tag 'application', media: 'all', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %> # Change this <%= javascript_pack_tag 'application', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %>

然后，它应该如下所示：

 <%= stylesheet_pack_tag 'application', media: 'all', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %> # After changing <%= javascript_pack_tag 'application', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %>

注意：“应用程序”是您的<filename>

在app/javascript中导入引导程序

在app/javascript创建一个文件夹stylesheets并在stylesheets文件夹中创建application.scss

 // The file structure should look like this // Note application.scss can be changed to <filename.scss> app |_ javascript |_ stylesheets |_ application.scss

然后，在文件中导入引导程序

// In app/javascript/stylesheets/application.scss @import "bootstrap"

然后，将以下代码放在app/javascript/packs/application.js文件的底部

// In app/javascript/packs/application.js import "bootstrap" import "../stylesheets/application" // Remember to change it to <filename>

现在，让我们进行测试

在app/views/layouts/application.html.erb中，将以下代码放入您的<body>标记和<%= yield %>标记之前。

 <body> <nav class="navbar navbar-expand-lg navbar-light bg-light"> <div class="container-fluid"> <a class="navbar-brand" href="#">Navbar</a> <button class="navbar-toggler" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#navbarSupportedContent" aria-controls="navbarSupportedContent" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation"> <span class="navbar-toggler-icon"></span> </button> <div class="collapse navbar-collapse" id="navbarSupportedContent"> <ul class="navbar-nav me-auto mb-2 mb-lg-0"> <li class="nav-item"> <a class="nav-link active" aria-current="page" href="#">Home</a> </li> <li class="nav-item"> <a class="nav-link" href="#">Link</a> </li> <li class="nav-item dropdown"> <a class="nav-link dropdown-toggle" href="#" id="navbarDropdown" role="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false"> Dropdown </a> <ul class="dropdown-menu" aria-labelledby="navbarDropdown"> <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="#">Action</a></li> <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="#">Another action</a></li> <li><hr class="dropdown-divider"></li> <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="#">Something else here</a></li> </ul> </li> <li class="nav-item"> <a class="nav-link disabled">Disabled</a> </li> </ul> <form class="d-flex"> <input class="form-control me-2" type="search" placeholder="Search" aria-label="Search"> <button class="btn btn-outline-success" type="submit">Search</button> </form> </div> </div> </nav> <%= yield %> </body>

检查导航栏（下拉菜单等）是否正常工作。

干杯〜快乐编码！



原文:

Let's first upgrade Rails to v7

Upgrade Rails to v7 

// In Gemfile

gem 'rails', '~> 7.0.0' # Change to this

// In the terminal

$ bundle update

Upgrade rails packages

In package.json, update all rails packages

$ yarn upgrade @rails/actioncable --latest
$ yarn upgrade @rails/activestorage --latest
$ yarn upgrade @rails/ujs --latest
$ yarn upgrade @rails/webpacker --latest

Run update rails task 

// In terminal

$ bin/rails app:update

Verify framework defaults 

// In config/application.rb

# old
config.load_defaults 6.1 # Change to 7.0

# new
config.load_defaults 7.0 # After change to 7.0

If you encountered this error

PG::UndefinedTable: ERROR: relation "active_storage_blobs" does not exist

Then do the following:

// In terminal

$ rails active_storage:install

It will create a create_active_storage_tables.active_storage.rb file in db/migrate, then

// In terminal

$ rails db:migrate

Then, go back to Verify framework defaults section, and proceed.
Read this stackoverflow solution

Now, we will proceed to install bootstrap v5.

Install bootstrap and popperjs/core to dependencies 

// In terminal

$ yarn add bootstrap and popperjs/core

So, your package.json file should have these 2 lines added:

"dependencies": {
    "@popperjs/core": "^2.11.5",
    "bootstrap": "^5.1.3"
}

Note: The versions here might be different but since our purpose is to install bootstrap v5, it must be something similar to 5.x.x.

Change extract_css in default to true 

// In config/webpacker.yml

default: &default
  extract_css: true # Change to true

Change stylesheet_link_tag to stylesheet_pack_tag

Since the original app is still with config of Rails v6, we would use Webpacker instead of Sprockets/Asset Pipeline

// In app/views/layouts/<filename>.html.erb
// My <filename> is application, so in app/views/layouts/application.html.erb

<%= stylesheet_link_tag 'application', media: 'all', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %> # Change this
<%= javascript_pack_tag 'application', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %>

Then, it should look like this:

<%= stylesheet_pack_tag 'application', media: 'all', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %> # After changing
<%= javascript_pack_tag 'application', 'data-turbolinks-track': 'reload' %>

Note: The 'application' is your <filename>

Import bootstrap in app/javascript

In app/javascript create a folder stylesheets and create application.scss in the stylesheets folder

// The file structure should look like this
// Note application.scss can be changed to <filename.scss>
app
 |_ javascript
     |_ stylesheets
         |_ application.scss

Then, import the bootstrap in the file

// In app/javascript/stylesheets/application.scss

@import "bootstrap"

Then, put the following code in the bottom of the app/javascript/packs/application.js file

// In app/javascript/packs/application.js

import "bootstrap"
import "../stylesheets/application"

// Remember to change it to <filename>

Now, let's do testing

In app/views/layouts/application.html.erb, put the following code in your <body> tag and before the <%= yield %> tag.

<body>
    <nav class="navbar navbar-expand-lg navbar-light bg-light">
      <div class="container-fluid">
        <a class="navbar-brand" href="#">Navbar</a>
        <button class="navbar-toggler" type="button" data-bs-toggle="collapse" data-bs-target="#navbarSupportedContent" aria-controls="navbarSupportedContent" aria-expanded="false" aria-label="Toggle navigation">
          <span class="navbar-toggler-icon"></span>
        </button>
        <div class="collapse navbar-collapse" id="navbarSupportedContent">
          <ul class="navbar-nav me-auto mb-2 mb-lg-0">
            <li class="nav-item">
              <a class="nav-link active" aria-current="page" href="#">Home</a>
            </li>
            <li class="nav-item">
              <a class="nav-link" href="#">Link</a>
            </li>
            <li class="nav-item dropdown">
              <a class="nav-link dropdown-toggle" href="#" id="navbarDropdown" role="button" data-bs-toggle="dropdown" aria-expanded="false">
                Dropdown
              </a>
              <ul class="dropdown-menu" aria-labelledby="navbarDropdown">
                <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="#">Action</a></li>
                <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="#">Another action</a></li>
                <li><hr class="dropdown-divider"></li>
                <li><a class="dropdown-item" href="#">Something else here</a></li>
              </ul>
            </li>
            <li class="nav-item">
              <a class="nav-link disabled">Disabled</a>
            </li>
          </ul>
          <form class="d-flex">
            <input class="form-control me-2" type="search" placeholder="Search" aria-label="Search">
            <button class="btn btn-outline-success" type="submit">Search</button>
          </form>
        </div>
      </div>
    </nav>
    <%= yield %>
</body>

Check whether the navbar (dropdown etc) is working.

Cheers~ Happy Coding!
item.author: michellelwt

item.title: SwiftUI 100 天 - WordScramble（项目 5） | 100 Days of SwiftUI - WordScramble (Project 5)
item.description:
这个应用程序是一个游戏，它会向玩家展示一个随机的八个字母的单词，并要求他们从中造出单词。 这个应用程序还引入了List 、 onAppear() 、 Bundle 、 fatalError()等。

列表

您还可以在同一个 List 元素中集成静态和动态内容

List { Section ( header : Text ( "Section 1" )) { Text ( "Static row 1" ) Text ( "Static row 2" ) } Section ( header : Text ( "Section 2" )) { ForEach ( 0 ..< 5 ) { Text ( "Dynamic row \( $0 ) " ) } } Section ( header : Text ( "Section 3" )) { Text ( "Static row 3" ) Text ( "Static row 4" ) } }

从您的应用程序包中加载资源

每当我们想要在应用程序的 Bundle 中用代码处理某些内容时，我们首先需要使用 URL 来定位它。 如果我们想读取主应用程序包中文件的 URL，我们使用 Bundle.main.url()。 如果文件存在，它将被发送回给我们，否则我们将返回 nil，所以这是一个可选的 URL。

 if let fileURL = Bundle . main . url ( forResource : "some-file" , withExtension : "txt" ) { // we found the file in our bundle! if let fileContents = try ? String ( contentsOf : fileURL ) { // we loaded the file into a string! } }

应用启动时运行代码

使用 UITextChecker 验证单词

为了检查一个单词是否是“有效的英语”，我们可以使用UITextChecker 。

 func isReal ( word : String ) -> Bool { let checker = UITextChecker () let range = NSRange ( location : 0 , length : word . utf16 . count ) let misspelledRange = checker . rangeOfMisspelledWord ( in : word , range : range , startingAt : 0 , wrap : false , language : "en" ) return misspelledRange . location == NSNotFound }

挑战

  1. 不允许少于三个字母或只是我们的起始词的答案。
  2. 添加一个调用startGame()的工具栏按钮，以便用户可以随时使用新单词重新开始。
  3. 在某处放置一个文本视图，以便您可以跟踪和显示玩家对给定词根的分数。 你如何计算分数取决于你，但涉及单词数量和字母数的东西是合理的。

输出

源代码

单词争夺

https://www.hackingwithswift.com/100/swiftui/29 https://www.hackingwithswift.com/100/swiftui/29



原文:

This app is a game that will show players a random eight-letter word, and ask them to make words out of it. This app also introduced List, onAppear(), Bundle, fatalError(), and others.

List

List is used to provide a scrolling table of data, much like Form. You can also integrate static and dynamic content within the same List element

List { Section(header: Text("Section 1")) { Text("Static row 1") Text("Static row 2") } Section(header: Text("Section 2")) { ForEach(0..<5) { Text("Dynamic row \($0)") } } Section(header: Text("Section 3")) { Text("Static row 3") Text("Static row 4") }
}

Loading resources from your app bundle

Whenever we have something in our app's Bundle that we want to deal with in code, we first need to locate it with a URL. If we want to read the URL for a file in our main app bundle, we use Bundle.main.url(). If the file exists it will be sent back to us, otherwise we’ll get back nil, so this is an optional URL.

if let fileURL = Bundle.main.url(forResource: "some-file", withExtension: "txt") { // we found the file in our bundle! if let fileContents = try? String(contentsOf: fileURL) { // we loaded the file into a string! }
}

Running code when app launches

SwiftUI gives us a dedicated view modifier onAppear() for running a closure when a view is shown.

.onAppear(perform: functionToRun)

Validating words with UITextChecker

In order to check if a word is "valid English", we can use UITextChecker.

func isReal(word: String) -> Bool { let checker = UITextChecker() let range = NSRange(location: 0, length: word.utf16.count) let misspelledRange = checker.rangeOfMisspelledWord(in: word, range: range, startingAt: 0, wrap: false, language: "en") return misspelledRange.location == NSNotFound
}

Challenge

  1. Disallow answers that are shorter than three letters or are just our start word.
  2. Add a toolbar button that calls startGame(), so users can restart with a new word whenever they want to.
  3. Put a text view somewhere so you can track and show the player’s score for a given root word. How you calculate score is down to you, but something involving number of words and their letter count would be reasonable.

Output

Source Code

WordScramble

https://www.hackingwithswift.com/100/swiftui/29
item.author: johnkevinlosito

item.title: 视频游戏如何随着时间演变 | How Video Gaming Evolved Over Time
item.description:
大量调查表明，越来越多的人选择游戏作为他们最喜欢的消遣方式。 试想一下：2020 年有 26.9 亿玩家。到 2023 年底，它将打破其 30.7 亿的全球新纪录。 而且这项业务不仅有利可图。 该行业似乎并没有放缓：我们将见证它的进一步增长。

电子游戏是什么时候开始的？ 它们是如何起源的？ 他们并不总是像我们所知道的那样。 流血的逼真图形，引人入胜的故事，点击几下即可获得……游戏发展非常迅速，其显着进步几乎无法与其他任何东西相提并论。 这种娱乐形式直到 20 世纪下半叶才出现。 但是，当这项技术确实出现时，它的进一步发展发生了空前的发展。

让我们画出这条进化之路，标记球体的关键事件。 我们将介绍一切是如何开始的以及它当时和现在的样子。

游戏概念的诞生

电子游戏存在多久了？ 这个庞大的娱乐业务的历史始于 1940 年。 它会在 90% 的时间内击败人类。 这种简单的组合挑战可以称为最古老的游戏。 有些人开始明白程序可以用于娱乐。

在 1950 年的时间线中，程序员开发了简单的电子玩具。 例如国际象棋、井字游戏和网球。 现代观众不会觉得它们有趣。 但他们在当时达到了他们的目的：他们为成为一种文化现象铺平了道路。

在第 60 届中，关键事件包括 2 个里程碑：

它是多人游戏，并迅速流行起来。
一年后，发明者开发了一款名为 Brown Box 的游戏机原型。 工作原理启发了美国电子公司 Magnavox 制造了类似的设备。 仅仅 4 年后，Odyssey 就成为了它生产的第一款游戏机：多人游戏和多程序。

一旦笨重的计算机变得更小，它们就会被放在 6 英尺高的柜子里。 他们主要登陆酒吧、购物中心和其他公共场所，吸引和娱乐人群。 那是街机的黄金时代。 最早在商业上成功的投币式机器之一是 Pong。 它创建于 1972 年，很快就进入了美国的许多城市。 这个玩具到处都是新闻。 这使它成为播放次数最多的游戏之一。

它使用了基于瓷砖的线框图形和其他射击游戏将采用的特色机制。 厄运包括。

市场领导者不断设计最新、最具吸引力和引人入胜的机器：

  • 在此期间，美国充斥着电子游戏厅。 人们玩得发疯了，而且总是想要更多。 一些城市甚至通过了法律，对放置娱乐设备的位置进行了限制。

    但控制台革命开始悄悄地获得力量。 但最重要的是，它支持带有彩色图像的标题。 该系统几乎进入了每个美国人的家中。

    控制台接管

    街机在 80 年代初仍然很流行。 该游戏被移植到几乎所有已知的系统。 它为庞大的特许经营权奠定了基础。

    甚至 Atari 2600 也发布了没人愿意购买的游戏。 墨盒就像垃圾一样被扔掉。 这是日本公司（如任天堂、世嘉等）成功进入市场的时候。

    任天堂在 1985 年创造了 NES，8 位家庭设备，然后在 1989 年创造了 Game Boy。后者是一款设计方便、游戏流畅、电池寿命长的手持设备。

    至于里程碑玩具，值得标记其中的两个：

    任天堂和其他制造商将它添加到每个控制台。
    其他标志性的角色扮演幻想是龙与地下城和休闲装拉里，成人热门角色扮演游戏。

    激烈的游戏机大战和 PC 游戏的发展

    控制台系统的领导者世嘉在 1991 年创造了一个标志性的英雄。刺猬索尼克是推动公司销售的最受欢迎的角色。 然而，它的结局不可避免地越来越近了。

    虽然它在销量上没有击败世嘉，但已经非常接近了。 一年后，任天堂 64 进一步削弱了领导者。 当 PlayStation 2 于 2000 年上市时，世嘉就死了。

    与此同时，PC 开始进入游戏市场，微软将纸牌游戏添加到系统中（1990 年）。 一年后，《魔兽争霸：兽人和人类》发布了相同的操作系统。 在此期间，由于数字玩具中的暴力行为增加，建立了评级系统。 人们希望限制孩子访问过于激进的内容。

    机器变得比人类更聪明。 现在让我们从1990年跳到2000年，见证齿轮的真正进步。

    技术变得更好

    技术进步推动了游戏的发展。 显卡、处理器和 CPU 变得更加先进。 性能冲击使数字世界更接近现实视觉的现代标准。 查看这篇文章，了解技术如何影响当今的行业。

    它的进一步修改配备了更好的图形传输设备。 在此期间，我们购买冒险的方式也发生了变化。 玩家们也见证了MMORPG的进步。 魔兽世界成为在线娱乐选择中的热门，2008 年拥有 10 多万粉丝。

    至于任天堂，它继续主导着手持设备行业。 它的 2004 DS 控制器是最常购买的游戏装备。 索尼也走这条路，制造了 PSP (2004) 和 PlayStation Vita (2011)。 但任天堂在其 2006 年的 Wii 中加入了革命性的元素。 它引入了动作并鼓励球员离开他们的教练。 例如，在 Wii Sports 中，控制器变成了球拍、球棒和其他东西。 该公司接触了那些永远不会称自己为游戏玩家的人。

    当视频标题在手机上可用时，移动市场开始获得力量。 后来，开发人员会创建许多令人上瘾的人群聚集游戏，例如 Clash of Clans 或 Candy Crush。

    快速变化继续

    独立、在线和 IO

    几年后，Agar.io 和 Slither.io 等浏览器驱动的冒险游戏重塑了多人游戏的意义。 他们缩短了回合，允许玩家在线移动，并引入了简单的游戏玩法。 它彻底炸毁了互联网。 如今，人们可以找到很多IO 游戏并从任何设备访问它们。

    增强现实/虚拟现实

    增强现实在 2011 年随着 Skylanders: Spyro's Adventures 的发布首次展示了它的潜力。 命中允许玩家使用街道作为游戏环境，这是一种轰动。 以及发展的新趋势。

    成熟的故事

    与此同时，电子游戏改变了社会及其价值观。 观众开始渴望成熟和情感的故事。 头衔不仅提供了您在现实生活中无法做到的事情。 他们还将您与具有艰难和道德驱动选择的角色联系在一起。 电子竞技

电子竞技诞生于 1990 年代，但在 2015 年变成了一项严肃的业务。那是 Twitch 开始蓬勃发展的时期。 它吸引了数百万观众观看电子锦标赛。 职业电竞选手在Fortnite （DOTA 2、使命召唤等）中相互竞争。 据Newzoo 称，2022 年全球电子竞技收入将超过 13.8 亿美元。

包起来

游戏随着时间的推移发生了怎样的变化？ 现在您可以跟踪开发并进行比较。 当时和现在有很大的不同。 新旧流派改变，更便捷的购买方式，甚至更进化的游戏玩法。 电脑玩具是当今的主流，任何玩家都有多种选择。 随着技术在各个方面变得更好，它将使它们更加现实和令人上瘾。 更多酷的电子游戏将会问世。 他们将进一步提升行业的氛围。 相信我们：你不会在未来十年内认出它！



原文:

Numerous surveys prove that more and more people choose gaming as their favorite pastime entertainment. Just imagine: there were 2.69 billion players in 2020. By the end of 2023, it’ll crush its new worldwide record of 3.07 billion. And this business is more than profitable. In 2022, global market revenue will achieve 188.13 billion, with smartphone-driven adventures dominating other platforms. The industry doesn’t seem to slow down: we’ll witness its further growth.

When did video games start? How did they originate? They have not always been as we know them. Bleeding edge realistic graphics, hooking stories, availability in a couple of clicks… Gaming evolved very fast, and its significant progress can hardly be compared to anything else. This entertainment form didn’t even exist until the latter half of the 20th century. But when the technology did appear, its further development happened by lips and bounds.

Let’s draw this evolution road, marking the key events for the sphere. We’ll cover how everything started and how it looked then and now.

Birth of the Gaming Concept

How long have video games been around? The history of this massive entertainment business started in the 1940th. Edward U. Condon made a program that can play Nim against a person. It would beat humans 90% of the time. This simple combinatorial challenge can be called the oldest game. Some began to understand that programs could be used for entertainment.

In the timeline of the 1950th, programmers developed simple electronic toys. Such as chess, tic-tac-toe, and tennis. A modern audience won’t find them interesting. But they served their purpose back then: they paved the way for becoming a cultural phenomenon.

1960s: First Steps in the Evolution of Video Games

In the 60th, the key events included 2 milestones:

In 1962, a student Steve Russell developed Spacewar!, a legendary shooter. It was multiplayer and quickly gained popularity.
In 1966, Ralph Baer suggested using TV screens for gaming. A year later, the inventor develops a prototype of a game console called Brown Box. The working principles inspired the American electronic company Magnavox to manufacture a similar device. Just 4 years later, Odyssey was the first console it produced: multi-player and multi-program.

1970s, Ten Years Later: Advancement of Arcade Machines and Consoles

Once bulky computers became smaller, they were put in 6-foot cabinets. They landed mostly in bars, shopping malls, and other public areas, attracting and entertaining crowds. It was the golden age of arcade machines. Manufacturers like Atari (the first company), Namco, Midway, and Taito made it to the market leaders.

Among the first commercially successful coin-operated machines was Pong. Created in 1972, it quickly found its way to many US cities. The toy was all over the news. 3 years later, Atari developed a home variant of this tennis arcade. Which made it one of the most played titles.

In 1974, the first networked FPS adventure was created – Maze Wars. It used tile-based wire-frame graphics and featured mechanics that would be adopted by other shooters. Doom including.

The market leaders kept designing the newest, most appealing, and engaging machines:

  • 1. In 1978, Taito presented Space Invaders with endless levels of alien shooting
  • 2. In 1979, Atari answered with Asteroids with multidirectional visuals
  • 3. In 1980, Namco released Pac-Man, an action maze chase challenge

During this period, the US was flooded with video arcades. People went crazy playing them and always came for more. Some cities even passed laws that set limitations on where to place the entertainment gear.

But the console revolution started to quietly gain its power. In 1977, Atari produced the Video Computer System – the legendary Atari 2600. It had a joystick, cartridges, and switches for choosing games/complexity levels. But most importantly, it supported titles with colored images. The system found its way to the home of almost every American.

Consoles Take Over

Arcade machines were still popular at the beginning of the 80th. In 1981, Nintendo created Donkey Kong, better known to us as Mario. The game was ported to almost every known system. It laid a foundation for a huge franchise.

In 1983, the market became oversaturated with low-quality console adventures. Even Atari 2600 released titles that no one wanted to buy. Cartridges were just thrown away like garbage. This is when Japanese companies (e.g. Nintendo, Sega, etc.) successfully entered the market.

Nintendo created NES, 8-bit home gear in 1985, and then Game Boy in 1989. The latter was a handheld device with a convenient design, smooth gameplay, and long battery life.

As for the milestone toys, it’s worth marking 2 of them:

In 1984, a Russian mathematician invented Tetris, a hooking puzzle challenge. Nintendo and other manufacturers added it to every console.
The Legend of Zelda became popular when it was released in 1987 by Nintendo. Other iconic role-playing fantasies are Dungeons&Dragons and Leisure Suit Larry, a hot RPG for adults.

Heated Console Wars and the Advancement of PC Gaming

A leader in console systems Sega created an iconic hero in 1991. Sonic the Hedgehog was a favorite character that boosted the company’s sales. However, its end was inevitably coming closer.

In 1995, Sony produced a cheaper device, PlayStation. Although it didn’t beat Sega in sales, it was very close to it. A year later, Nintendo 64 weakens the leader even more. When PlayStation 2 hit the market in 2000, Sega died.

Meanwhile, PCs started entering the gaming market, with Microsoft adding Solitaire to the system (1990). In 1994, Doom was designed for MS-DOS. A year later, Warcraft: Orcs and Humans was released for the same OS. During this period, a rating system was established due to increased violence in digital toys. People wanted to limit access to too aggressive content for kids.

Machines became cleverer than humans. In 1997, the supercomputer Deep Blue beat the brightest chess mind of that time – Gary Kasparov.

Now let’s jump from 1990 to 2000 to witness the true progress of gear.

Tech Gets Better

The evolution of gaming was boosted by technological progress. Graphics cards, processors, and CPUs got more advanced. Performance hit made digital worlds closer to modern standards of realistic visuals. Check out this article to see how technology influences the industry nowadays.

In 2001, Microsoft jumped into the competition by introducing Xbox. Its further modifications were equipped with gear for better graphics delivery. Xbox 360 opened new opportunities for online play. Xbox Live was meant for a multiplayer experience.

During this time, the way we bought adventures also changed. Valve launched its Steam for digital distribution: it strengthened the PC positions. Players also witnessed the advancement of MMORPG. World of Warcraft became a hit among online entertainment options, with 10+ million followers in 2008.

As for Nintendo, it continued to dominate the industry of handheld devices. Its 2004 DS controller was the most often bought gaming gear. Sony also went this way, manufacturing the PSP (2004) and PlayStation Vita (2011). But Nintendo added revolutionary elements to its 2006 Wii. It introduced movements and encouraged players off their coaches. For example, with Wii Sports, the controller turned into a racket, bat, and other things. The company reached out to people who would never call themselves gamers.

When video titles became available on phones, the mobile market started to gain power. Angry Birds and Farmville received massive revenues in 2009. It was clear back then: mobile devices were a promising sphere. Later, developers would create lots of addictive crowd-gatherers like Clash of Clans or Candy Crush.

Rapid Changes Go On

Indie, Online, and IO

The market exploded when an indie creation Minecraft became a popular phenomenon in 2010. Sandbox multiplayer title had simple block-based visuals and no limitations for creativity. A few years later, browser-driven adventures like Agar.io and Slither.io reshaped the meaning of multiplayer matches. They made rounds short, allowed players to move online, and introduced simple gameplay. It totally blew the Internet. Nowadays, one can find lots of IO games and access them from any device.

AR/VR

Augmented reality first showed its potential in 2011 with the release of Skylanders: Spyro’s Adventures. 5 years later, Pokemon Go united our and digital worlds. The hit allowed players to use streets as a playing environment, which was a sensation. As well as a new trend to develop.

Mature Stories

At the same time, video games changed society and its values. The audience started to crave mature and emotional stories. Titles didn’t only offer to do something you can’t do in real life. They also tied you to characters with hard and moral-driven choices. The Last of Us (2013) is a bright example and among the best action adventures.

Esports

Esports was born in the 1990s, but it turned into a serious business in 2015. It was the time when Twitch started to bloom. It attracted millions of viewers to watch e-championships. Professional esportsmen competed with each other in Fortnite (DOTA 2, Call of Duty, etc.). According to Newzoo, global esports revenue will be over $1.38 billion in 2022.

Wrapping Up

How has gaming changed over time? Now you can track the development and make comparisons. Then and now are very different. Altered old and new genres, more convenient purchasing methods, and even much-evolved gameplay. Computer toys are mainstream today, with a variety of options for any player. As technology gets better in every way, it’ll make them even more realistic and addictive. More cool video games will come out. They’ll further boost the vibe of the industry. Trust us: you won’t recognize it in the next ten years!
item.author: anna_yurovskikh

item.title: 前端新闻#062 | Chrome 101, MDN Plus updates, Brave 1.38, Edge 101, Node v16.15.0 (LTS), React 18.1.0, and more | Front End News #062
item.description:
注意：这是我的时事通讯的第 062 期，于 5 月 2 日星期一上线。 如果您发现此信息有用且有趣，并且希望在发布后收到未来的问题，那么我邀请您加入frontendnexus.com上的订阅者列表。

在更多浏览器新闻中，Brave 1.38 和 Microsoft Edge 101 也在过去一周发布。 软件发布部分包含 Node v16.15.0 (LTS)、React 18.1.0 等。

现在任何人都可以创建 MDN Plus 免费帐户。 您将能够接收来自最多三个选定页面的通知，并且最多可以保存 5 篇文章。

现在还向另外 16 个国家/地区提供付费等级：奥地利、比利时、芬兰、法国、英国、德国、爱尔兰、意大利、马来西亚、荷兰、新西兰、波多黎各、瑞典、新加坡、瑞士和西班牙。

  • 浏览器新闻

有两个稳定的发布 - Chrome 101 和 Firefox 99 - 以及三个 beta 更新 - Chrome 102、Firefox 100 和 Safari 15.5。 这篇文章不包含任何其他基于 Chromium 的浏览器的详细信息，即使在 Google Chrome 中启动的功能不一定适用于其他浏览器，例如 Microsoft Edge、Brave 或 Vivaldi。

  • 四月新加入网络平台

勇敢的

勇敢发布更新 1.38。 更新的 Brave Shields 面板有助于改善您的隐私。 您可以选择始终跳过 AMP 页面并直接访问源代码。 铬合金

令人惊讶的是，开发者博客上没有包含发行说明的文章。 但是，我们仍然从其他来源（例如上面 Rachel Andrew 的文章或下面链接的 Chrome Status 路线图板）获得了有关更改的提示。

此更新中有两个主要功能。 第一个是对hwb()颜色表示法的支持。 第二个是新的优先级提示功能，它允许您向浏览器建议它应该下载资源的顺序。

开发人员现在可以在样式面板中查看层叠层。 他们还收到了使用hwb()颜色模式的工具。

  • 边缘

    他们的发行说明似乎也更针对系统管理员。 因此，Edge 101 具有客户端证书切换器、从收藏夹栏中启动渐进式 Web 应用程序 (PWA)的选项，或对企业站点列表管理器的改进。 还有一长串新政策，但我不会去那个兔子洞。

    • 软件更新和发布

      收拾东西

      乌克兰仍在遭受俄罗斯入侵 - 如果您正在寻找帮助的方法，请查看 Smashing Magazine 的文章We All Are Ukraine 🇺🇦或与您信任的慈善机构联系。

      这就是我对这个问题的全部了解。 如果您喜欢本时事通讯，有几种方法可以支持它。 您可以在社交媒体上分享此问题的链接，并在Twitter 上关注此时事通讯。 这些都对我有帮助，我会很感激你的考虑。

      祝您度过愉快而富有成效的一周，确保自己的安全，尽可能多地与您所爱的人共度时光，我们下次再见！

NOTE: This is issue #062 of my newsletter, which went live on Monday, May 2nd. If you find this information useful and interesting and you want to receive future issues as they are published, ahead of everyone else, I invite you to join the subscriber list at frontendnexus.com.

The Chrome 101 update is rolling out to users out there. The MDN Plus program has received updates, with anyone being able to create a free account, while the premium option is available in 16 more countries. In more browser news, Brave 1.38 and Microsoft Edge 101 have also been released over the past week. And the software release section features Node v16.15.0 (LTS), React 18.1.0, and more.

MDN Plus now available in more countries

Mozilla released new updates to their MDN Plus program. Anyone can now create an MDN Plus free account. You will be able to receive notifications from up to three selected pages and can save a maximum of 5 articles.

The paid tiers are also now offered to 16 more countries: Austria, Belgium, Finland, France, United Kingdom, Germany, Ireland, Italy, Malaysia, the Netherlands, New Zealand, Puerto Rico, Sweden, Singapore, Switzerland, and Spain.

  • MDN Plus now available in more countries

Browser news

Rachel Andrew published another roundup of the new web platform updates that landed in April. There are two stable launches - Chrome 101 and Firefox 99 - and three beta updates - Chrome 102, Firefox 100, and Safari 15.5. The article does not include any details about any other Chromium-based browsers, even though the features that are launched in Google Chrome don't necessarily make it to the other browsers, such as Microsoft Edge, Brave, or Vivaldi.

  • New to the web platform in April

Brave

Brave released update 1.38. The updated Brave Shields panel helps improve your privacy. You have the option to always skip AMP pages and go straight to the source. And Gemini users can now connect to the Brave wallet.

Chrome

Chrome 101 launched this last week. Surprisingly, there is no article on the developer blog with the release notes. However, we still got hints of the changes from other sources (like Rachel Andrew's article above or the Chrome Status roadmap board linked below).

Two major features landed in this update. The first one is the support for hwb() color notation. The second one is the new Priority hints feature, which allows you to suggest to your browser the order in which it should download resources.

Developers can now view Cascade Layers in the Styles panel. They also received the tools to use the hwb() color mode.

  • Chrome Status Roadmap
  • What's New In DevTools (Chrome 101)

Edge

Microsoft Edge keeps with its policy of launching updates silently, without much announcement. Their release notes also seem targeted more towards system admins. As such Edge 101 features a Client Certificate Switcher, the option to Launch Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) from the favorites bar, or Improvements to the Enterprise Site List Manager. There is also a long list of new policies, but I am not going down that rabbit hole.

  • Microsoft Edge v101 Release Notes

Software updates and releases

Wrapping things up

Ukraine is still suffering from the Russian invasion - if you are looking for ways to help, please check Smashing Magazine's article We All Are Ukraine 🇺🇦 or get in touch with your trusted charity.

That's about all I have for this issue. If you enjoyed this newsletter, there are a couple of ways to support it. You can share the link to this issue on social media and follow this newsletter on Twitter. Each of these helps me out, and I would appreciate your consideration.

Have a great and productive week, keep yourselves safe, spend as much time as possible with your loved ones, and I will see you again next time!

item.title: 你最糟糕的编程经历是什么？ | What was your worst programming experience?
item.description:
生命编码器

发表于我正在编写 2000 行代码来构建一个网站，却忘记了保存，你知道发生了什么，对吧？

弗拉基米尔·马哈切克 - May 1 5月1日



原文:

lifeascoder

Posted on May 2

Mine was writing 2000 lines of code to build a website and forgot to save and you know what happened, right?

Vladimír Macháček - May 1
item.author: coderlifeisawesome

item.title: 金融科技在后 COVID-19 世界中的作用 | Role of FinTech in the post-COVID-19 world
item.description:
弹性、机会和创新

但大多数金融科技公司也表现出韧性和应对财务困难的能力，让他们有希望在危机中幸存下来。 他们高水平的股权融资，加上敏捷的运营和接受远程工作的意愿，使金融科技公司能够抵御颠覆。 他们还采取措施减少风险敞口并削减成本，包括裁员。

根据 Beauhurst 的说法，只有 1% 的金融科技公司受到 COVID-19 的严重影响，2% 的公司受到严重影响。 相比之下，大约 17% 的其他高增长公司属于这些类别。

随着 COVID-19 时代工作实践和客户银行业务习惯的改变，许多金融科技公司的需求激增。 技术不断改变金融服务行业的面貌。 例如，与传统金融服务相比，数字金融服务的出现创造了更快、更高效且通常更便宜的银行业务。

在疫情爆发之前，很明显金融科技将在未来的金融服务中发挥关键作用。 正在重新评估商业模式，以在 COVID-19 后的世界中纳入新战略和远程功能。

 “Prior to the outbreak, it was clear that FinTech would play a pivotal role in financial services going forward. COVID-19 has undoubtedly accelerated that process.”

老牌金融机构被迫加快其数字化计划以满足新的需求。 随着基础设施需求的发展，自动化和其他数字服务变得越来越重要，金融科技公司已准备好提供创新的解决方案。

事实上，该行业对危机并不陌生。 金融科技浪潮主要源于 2007/08 年全球金融危机 (GFC) 造成的混乱。 不确定性孕育创新，在全球金融危机之后，金融科技吸引了大量投资，并开始彻底改变金融服务。 数字金融

随着全球经济从 COVID-19 中复苏，金融科技的一个特别关注领域是金融包容性。 据世界银行称，目前全球约有 17 亿没有银行账户的人，金融科技将成为将这些人融入全球银行体系的核心。

这样做将有助于减轻大流行的经济和社会影响。 据德勤称，金融科技与跨辖区的金融机构、零售商和政府部门建立战略合作伙伴关系，可以通过以公平和透明的方式向经济弱势群体提供基本金融服务来帮助金融服务民主化。

数字金融也在其他领域扩张。 尽管预计现金使用无论如何都会下降，但 COVID-19 加速了这种下降，因为担心交出钱会导致病毒在人与人之间传播。 根据万事达卡对冠状病毒大流行影响的调查，全球 82% 的受访者认为非接触式支付是更清洁的支付方式，74% 的受访者表示他们将在大流行后继续使用非接触式支付。

此外，需要改进“了解您的客户”服务，以应对自疫情爆发以来数字欺诈和网络犯罪的增加。 随着越来越多的全球经济和金融系统继续在线移动，网络防御对于保护数据将变得更加重要。 具有安全意识的金融科技公司在设计他们的产品时考虑到了这一点——即使面对面的会议和流程确实会回归。

鉴于对技术的日益依赖，数字化转型将成为未来数月和数年金融机构的主要关注点。 尽管以前整合技术的努力可能范围有限，但许多金融机构正在意识到，为了提供高效、有效和可持续的银行服务，他们必须采用更全面的数字化转型方法，包括利用金融科技。

Original Article 来源文章



原文:

Resilience, opportunity and innovation

But most FinTechs have also demonstrated resilience and an ability to handle financial difficulties, giving them hope of surviving the crisis intact. Their high level of equity finance, coupled with agile operations and a willingness to embrace remote working, has enabled FinTechs to withstand the disruption. They have also taken steps to reduce their risk exposure and cut costs, including workforce reduction.

According to Beauhurst, only 1 percent of FinTechs have been critically affected by COVID-19 and 2 percent severely affected. By comparison, around 17 percent of other high-growth companies fall into these categories.

Many FinTechs have experienced a surge in demand as working practices and customer banking habits change in the COVID-19 era. Technology continues to change the face of the financial services industry. The advent of digital financial services, for example, has created faster, more efficient and typically cheaper banking compared to traditional financial services.

Prior to the outbreak, it was clear that FinTech would play a pivotal role in financial services going forward. COVID-19 has undoubtedly accelerated that process. Business models are being re-evaluated to incorporate new strategies and remote capabilities in a post-COVID-19 world.

“Prior to the outbreak, it was clear that FinTech would play a pivotal role in financial services going forward. COVID-19 has undoubtedly accelerated that process.”

Established FIs have been forced to expedite their digitalisation plans to meet new demands. As their infrastructure needs have evolved, automation and other digital services have become more important, with FinTechs ready to deliver innovative solutions.

Indeed, the industry is no stranger to crisis. The FinTech wave emerged primarily from the chaos caused by the 2007/08 global financial crisis (GFC). Uncertainty breeds innovation, and in the wake of the GFC, FinTech attracted significant investment and began to revolutionise financial services. COVID-19 presents another opportunity to advance this legacy.

Digital finance

As the global economy recovers from COVID-19, one particular area of focus for FinTech is financial inclusion. According to the World Bank, there are currently around 1.7 billion unbanked individuals worldwide, and FinTechs will be central to efforts to integrate these people into the global banking system.

Doing so will help to mitigate the economic and social impact of the pandemic. According to Deloitte, FinTechs, in strategic partnerships with financial institutions, retailers and government sectors across jurisdictions, can help democratise financial services by providing basic financial services in a fair and transparent way to economically vulnerable populations.

Digital finance is also expanding in other areas. Health concerns in the COVID-19 era have made physical cash payments less practical, opening the door to an increase in digital payments and e-wallets. Though cash use was predicted to decline in any case, COVID-19 has hurried that decline, due to concerns that handing over money can cause human to human transmission of the virus. According to a Mastercard survey looking at the implications of the coronavirus pandemic, 82 percent of respondents worldwide viewed contactless as the cleaner way to pay, and 74 percent said they will continue to use contactless payment post-pandemic.

In addition, improved ‘know your customer’ services are required to counter the rise in digital fraud and cyber crime since the beginning of the outbreak. As more of the global economic and financial system continues to move online, cyber defences will become even more crucial to protect data. Security-conscious FinTechs are designing their products with this in mind – even if face-to-face meetings and processes do return.

Given the rising reliance on technology, digital transformation will be a primary focus for FIs in the months and years ahead. Whereas previous efforts to integrate technology may have been limited in scope, many FIs are awakening to the fact that in order to deliver an efficient, effective and sustainable banking service, they must adopt a more holistic approach to digital transformation, which includes utilising FinTech.

Original Article

item.title: 持续交付的商业利益 | Business Benefits of Continuous Delivery
item.description:
从每个项目的开始，一直到整个过程，每个开发者都期待着软件或相关更新最终发布的那一天。
如果发布的代码没有任何错误，等待会更加富有成效。 这意味着让代码通过大量的测试，确保代码没有错误。

通过敏捷和DevOps等方法，这导致了软件开发机制从瀑布模型向连续性的演变。 今天我们看到一个黑白的区别（如果比较上面的模型），瀑布模型有不重叠的步骤，到现在敏捷和DevOps模型被定义为SDLC的重叠迭代过程。

连续模型意味着优质产品的频繁和可预测的发布。 如果每天都依赖管道，那么在整个开发周期中，围绕应用程序发布或其计划更新的风险将大大降低； 因为更容易发现错误并快速解决它们。 因此，使管道健壮，产品发布更顺畅。

持续交付包含持续集成、持续测试、持续监控和管道分析； 在强调持续交付的同时，我们的目标是让我们的技术团队响应不断变化的市场趋势。 这不仅仅是在“独角兽”公司、科技公司或大型企业中看到或执行的独家做法； 这也可以而且应该由小型初创公司执行或采用。

为了支持上述陈述，我们将研究一些业务案例，讨论实施持续交付的好处和方式。

持续交付的主要业务优势

提高交付速度

当谈到在市场上超越竞争对手时，最好的方法是提高您对市场趋势的响应能力。 为此，应该以适当的时间间隔安排假定的应用程序更新，或者可能应该更快地发布新软件，并且这些可以通过自动化软件交付管道轻松实现。 通过更快的上市时间，企业可以在这个竞争激烈的科技领域占据一席之地。

质量一直是任何组织真正取胜的决定性因素。 当我们说“速度”时，我们的意思是更快地交付最高质量。

错误代码的更快推出就像以自杀速度为目标，淹没了客户的负面评论的所有努力。

提高生产力

更高的生产力是成长的标志，也是快乐团队的副产品。 更快乐的团队意味着更多的参与，这为创新创造了空间。 如果繁琐的任务减少，幸福指数可以提高。

繁琐的任务可能像填写错误报告或进行测试并重复整个开发过程等。当这些任务自动化并发现所有错误时，以适当的格式记录下来，帮助开发团队识别并随后, 代码被更新，单元测试、代码审查和集成测试的整个过程都发生在一个自动化的管道中 - 只需花费最少的时间就可以解决问题。

支持可持续性

企业正在参加长程马拉松比赛。 保持领先很累。 为了保持领先地位，我们需要在我们的产品中带来充分的差异化，让它们脱颖而出。 除此之外，我们需要确保每个版本都没有任何可能的错误。

说起来容易做起来难！

要使上述几点成为现实，需要人们全天候工作。 但是，如果在重复性任务中实施自动化，工作量就会大大减少。 此外，从财务上讲，如果通过机器而不是人员完成某事，它的成本总是更低。
此外，综上所述，持续交付使企业具有灵活性，并使其更容易专注于业务的核心目标。

转向持续交付时的挑战

虽然持续交付是正确的做法，但设计有弹性的持续交付管道可不是小菜一碟！
构建这些管道涉及大量技术流程、运营文化和组织思维，在初始阶段可能看起来令人生畏。

低预算

创建持续交付管道需要开发团队中最优秀的成员。 公司，尤其是初创公司，发现很难分配高级工程师来开发这些管道，以保持其他优先事项的运行。 把它当作一个副项目，让初级团队参与进来不会有太大帮助，反正它又需要高级工程师的关注。 不足为奇，尽管为了获得更好的长期结果/增长，我们需要开始关注构建模块，或者可以帮助实现这一目标的重要要求。

唯一的解决方案是制定计划并适当地分配资金，其中团队可以生成可以在整个组织中扩展的持续交付管道 MVP（最小可行产品）。



原文:

From the start of every project, and throughout, every developer looks forward to the day, when the software or the related update will be finally released.
The wait is even more fruitful if the released code is devoid of any errors. That means making the code pass through a ceremonial load of tests, ensuring the code is free of bugs.

This has led to an evolution in the software development mechanism from the Waterfall Model toward continuity, through the methodologies like Agile and DevOps. Today we see a black and white difference (if the above models are compared), the Waterfall Model had non-overlapping steps, to now Agile and DevOps model is defined as an overlapping iterative process of SDLC.

The continuous model implies the frequent and predictable release of quality products. If relied upon a pipeline on daily basis, for the entire development cycle, the risks surrounding the release of applications or their scheduled updates are drastically reduced; as it is easier to notice errors and resolve them quickly. Thereby, making the pipeline robust and product releases smoother.

Continuous delivery encapsulates continuous integration, continuous testing, constant monitoring, and pipeline analytics; while emphasizing continuous delivery, we are aiming at making our tech teams responsive to changing market trends. It’s not just an exclusive practice seen or performed in “Unicorn” companies, tech companies, or large enterprises; this can also and should be performed or adopted by the small-scale startups too.

To support the above statement, we will be looking at some business cases, discussing the benefits and the ways Continuous Delivery can be implemented.

Top Business Benefits of Continuous Delivery

Improves Deliverability Speed

When it comes to outplaying your competitors in the market, the best way is to increase your responsiveness toward market trends. For that, the supposed application updates should be scheduled at proper intervals, or maybe new software should be released faster, and these can be easily facilitated with automated software delivery pipelines. With a faster time-to-market, businesses can claim a position in this very competitive Tech space.

Quality has always been the defining factor for any organization to truly win. When we say “speed,” we mean faster delivery of the highest quality.

Faster rollouts of erroneous code would be like targeting a suicidal speed, drowning all the efforts by the negative reviews from customers.

Enhanced Productivity

Better productivity is a sign of growth and is a byproduct of happy teams. Happier teams mean more engagement, which creates space for innovation. The happiness index can be made high if the amount of tedious tasks is reduced.

The tedious tasks can be like filling bug reports or conducting tests and repeating the entire process of development, etc. When tasks like these are automated, and all the errors found, get documented in an appropriate format, helping the dev team in recognition and subsequently, code is renewed, the entire process of unit testing, code review, and integration testing happens in an automated pipeline - the problem is just solved with least possible time invested.

Supports Sustainability

Businesses are in for long marathons. Staying ahead is very tiring. To stay ahead, we need to bring ample differentiation in our products, to make them stand out better. In addition to that, we need to ensure that every release is devoid of any possible errors.

Easier said than done!

What requires to make the above-mentioned points a reality, requires people to work 24/7. But if automation is implemented in repetitive tasks, the workload gets substantially reduced. Moreover, financially speaking, it always costs less, if something is done through machines rather than personnel.
Furthermore, with all that said and done, continuous delivery empowers businesses with flexibility and makes it easier to focus on the core objectives of the business.

Challenges while shifting to Continuous Delivery

Although continuous delivery is the right thing to do, designing resilient continuous delivery pipelines is no piece of cake!
Constructing these pipelines involves a huge deal of technical processes, operational culture, and organizational thinking, which can look daunting in the initial stages.

Low Budget

Creating continuous delivery pipelines requires the best players of the dev team. Companies, especially startups, find it difficult to allocate the senior engineers to the development of these pipelines, in order to keep their other priorities running. Treating it as a side project, and involving the junior team won’t be of much help, where anyway it would again require the attention of senior engineers. Not much surprise, though for better long-term results/growth, we need to start focusing on the building blocks, or the vital requirements that can help in this.

The only solution can be is to develop a plan and allocate funds appropriately, wherein the team can produce a continuous delivery pipeline MVP (minimum viable product) that can be scaled throughout your organization.
